notes: Chennai Super Kings innings
Powerplay 1: Overs 0.1 - 6.0 (Mandatory - 61 runs, 0 wicket)
Chennai Super Kings Impact Player Subs: Matthew Short, Kartik Sharma, Akeal Hosein, Prashant Veer and Matt Henry
Delhi Capitals Impact Player Subs: Ashutosh Sharma, Vipraj Nigam, Nitish Rana, Dushmantha Chameera and Karun Nair
Chennai Super Kings: 50 runs in 5.1 overs (31 balls), Extras 1
1st Wicket: 50 runs in 31 balls (SV Samson 35, RD Gaikwad 14, Ex 1)
SV Samson: 50 off 26 balls (9 x 4)
Strategic Timeout: Chennai Super Kings - 77/1 in 9.0 overs (SV Samson 52, A Mhatre 9)
Over 8.6: Review by Delhi Capitals (Bowling), Decision Challenged - Wide, Umpire - MV Saidharshan Kumar, Batter - SV Samson (Upheld)
Chennai Super Kings: 100 runs in 10.4 overs (64 balls), Extras 1
2nd Wicket: 50 runs in 32 balls (SV Samson 25, A Mhatre 24, Ex 1)
Strategic Timeout: Chennai Super Kings - 130/1 in 13.0 overs (SV Samson 77, A Mhatre 36)
A Mhatre: 50 off 27 balls (3 x 4, 4 x 6)
Chennai Super Kings: 150 runs in 14.3 overs (87 balls), Extras 2
2nd Wicket: 100 runs in 55 balls (SV Samson 45, A Mhatre 54, Ex 1)
SV Samson: 100 off 52 balls (14 x 4, 3 x 6)
Chennai Super Kings: 200 runs in 19.1 overs (115 balls), Extras 3
Innings Break: Chennai Super Kings - 212/2 in 20.0 overs (SV Samson 115, S Dube 20)
Delhi Capitals innings
Powerplay 1: Overs 0.1 - 6.0 (Mandatory - 66 runs, 2 wickets)
Chennai Super Kings Impact player Sub: Akeal Hosein in for Ayush Mhatre (Chennai Super Kings innings, 19.6 ov)
Kartik Sharma(sub) kept wickets in place of Sanju Samson from 0.1 to 2.6 ov
Delhi Capitals: 50 runs in 3.5 overs (25 balls), Extras 2
1st Wicket: 50 runs in 25 balls (P Nissanka 31, KL Rahul 18, Ex 2)
Strategic Timeout: Delhi Capitals - 82/4 in 9.0 overs (DA Miller 5, T Stubbs 3)
Delhi Capitals: 100 runs in 10.5 overs (67 balls), Extras 10
Delhi Capitals Impact Player Sub: Ashutosh Sharma in for Mukesh Kumar (Delhi Capitals innings, 12.2 ov)
Over 14.3: Review by Chennai Super Kings (Bowling), Decision Challenged - Wicket, Umpire - Vinod Seshan, Batter - AR Sharma (Struck down)
Strategic Timeout: Delhi Capitals - 149/6 in 14.5 overs (T Stubbs 35)
Delhi Capitals: 150 runs in 14.6 overs (92 balls), Extras 12
T Stubbs: 50 off 30 balls (2 x 4, 3 x 6)
Over 18.2: Review by Chennai Super Kings (Bowling), Decision Challenged - Wide, Umpire - Vinod Seshan, Batter - T Stubbs (Upheld)
Slow over-rate Penalty: Overs 19.1 - 20.0 (extra fielder inside the circle)

dismissalText.commentary: Ruturaj Gaikwad c Nissanka b Patel 15 (18b 1x4 0x6 32m) SR: 83.33

dismissalComment.html: miscued and taken! Axar puts Gaikwad out of his misery! A painstaking innings comes to an end. Back of a length on middle and leg. Skids through and Gaikwad, shaping for the pull, is rushed into it. Cloths it high up and deep mid wicket does the rest. Chepauk goes silent but from an immediate CSK POV, this might not be the worst outcome, given how much Gaikwad was struggling to up the tempo!

dismissalText.commentary: Axar Patel c Khan b Gurjapneet Singh 1 (2b 0x4 0x6 4m) SR: 50

dismissalComment.html: is that a bird, is that a plane?! No, it is Sarfaraz flying through the air! And a first-ball wicket for the left-arm seamer. Banged in short outside off. Axar cannot keep his cut down but he absolutely laces it towards backward point. Sarfaraz throws himself to his right and plucks it out of thin air. Runs around like he has just won a medal in the 100m event at the Olympics, and a moment of magic hands CSK their third scalp!

dismissalText.commentary: Tristan Stubbs c Noor Ahmad b Overton 60 (38b 4x4 3x6 64m) SR: 157.89

dismissalComment.html: plonked to mid off, and Overton is having a night to remember under the Chepauk lights! Hard length on middle and off, bowled at around 144 clicks. Stubbs looks to muscle it over mid off but the ball comes onto him much quicker than he expects. Off the higher part of the blade and Noor, running back from mid off, snares it. Overton is pumped, and over the moon. The crowd are too, and this could be the game right here!

dismissalText.commentary: Lungi Ngidi c Gurjapneet Singh b Kamboj 3 (5b 0x4 0x6 10m) SR: 60

dismissalComment.html: taken at short third, and that is a fitting end to this contest! CSK are on the board at the fourth time of asking and they have won at Chepauk for the first time in seven attempts! Pushed across the batter outside off. Ngidi wafts at it and the ball flies off the outside edge. Gobbled up neatly by Gurjapneet, and cue the celebrations now. More relief than anything, given the history of this franchise, and this has comfortably been their best performance this season!

dismissalText.commentary: KL Rahul c Overton b Ahmed 18 (10b 2x4 1x6 26m) SR: 180

dismissalComment.html: change of angle works immediately! Bashed into the track, and this slants into off. Rahul wants to swipe it over mid wicket but the lack of room means he cannot free his arms fully. A little rushed too and he splices it tamely to Overton at mid wicket. First wicket for Khaleel this season, and some respite for CSK!

dismissalText.commentary: Pathum Nissanka c Brevis b Kamboj 41 (24b 5x4 2x6 29m) SR: 170.83

dismissalComment.html: second time is the charm for Kamboj, and Chepauk comes to life! Khaleel breathes a sigh of relief, as do thousands clad in yellow at the stadium. Full on middle and off, and this shapes back in a shade. Holds in the surface too and Nissanka, checking his drive, pops it straight to Brevis. CSK getting their teeth stuck in now!

dismissalText.commentary: Axar Patel c Khan b Gurjapneet Singh 1 (2b 0x4 0x6 4m) SR: 50

dismissalComment.html: is that a bird, is that a plane?! No, it is Sarfaraz flying through the air! And a first-ball wicket for the left-arm seamer. Banged in short outside off. Axar cannot keep his cut down but he absolutely laces it towards backward point. Sarfaraz throws himself to his right and plucks it out of thin air. Runs around like he has just won a medal in the 100m event at the Olympics, and a moment of magic hands CSK their third scalp!

dismissalComment.html: goes a mile up again, and Brevis was not going to drop it. Not in a million years! This hit-the-deck ploy working wonders for the hosts. Just outside off. Rizvi looks to swivel and dump it over deep square leg. Miscues it right off the toe-end, though, and Brevis accepts the offering with glee, while stationed a few yards inside the fence. CSK in control now!

dismissalText.commentary: David Miller b Overton 17 (14b 1x4 1x6 37m) SR: 121.42

dismissalComment.html: hot knife through butter, and Overton is at it again! Hard length on leg, and this seems to deck back in just a smidgen. Miller has no clue about it. Beaten for pace (this was bowled at more than 145kph) and beaten by the movement too. Cut in half, and the leg-stump bail goes for a toss. Miller bewildered as he walks off, but this crowd is now having a ball at Chepauk!

dismissalText.commentary: Auqib Nabi c Hosein b Overton 4 (2b 1x4 0x6 4m) SR: 200

dismissalComment.html: Nabi swings for the hills, and he only finds Akeal at extra cover, who pulls off a stunning grab! Akeal did misjudge it at the start and had to redeem himself, much like Hetmyer yesterday. Length ball outside off. Nabi wanted to smash it into next week but miscued it terribly. Akeal ran in too much initially but then had the awareeness to leap and take it over his head. DC seven down now!

dismissalText.commentary: Tristan Stubbs c Noor Ahmad b Overton 60 (38b 4x4 3x6 64m) SR: 157.89

dismissalComment.html: plonked to mid off, and Overton is having a night to remember under the Chepauk lights! Hard length on middle and off, bowled at around 144 clicks. Stubbs looks to muscle it over mid off but the ball comes onto him much quicker than he expects. Off the higher part of the blade and Noor, running back from mid off, snares it. Overton is pumped, and over the moon. The crowd are too, and this could be the game right here!

dismissalText.commentary: Lungi Ngidi c Gurjapneet Singh b Kamboj 3 (5b 0x4 0x6 10m) SR: 60

dismissalComment.html: taken at short third, and that is a fitting end to this contest! CSK are on the board at the fourth time of asking and they have won at Chepauk for the first time in seven attempts! Pushed across the batter outside off. Ngidi wafts at it and the ball flies off the outside edge. Gobbled up neatly by Gurjapneet, and cue the celebrations now. More relief than anything, given the history of this franchise, and this has comfortably been their best performance this season!
